followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I lower the overall and technical-soundness scores modestly because the other reviews reinforced that the unpublished Ref. [19] is load-bearing. I also increase confidence after checking the HTML equation alt-text for the Maxwell ansatz and the Petrov paragraph. The main verdict remains contested rather than outright rejection because the concrete examples and local reductions still look meaningful.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The objection that the electromagnetic solution is actually a transverse magnetic field appears incorrect. Equation (15)/(21) gives A_mu = epsilon v lambda_mu and F proportional to dv wedge du, not F_xy.

The claim that the paper says the Kerr-Schild null vector lambda ceases to be null is a misreading. The norm statement after (57) concerns n_mu = delta_mu^v, whose full-metric norm is -2V; lambda remains null.

A separate Bianchi-identity objection is not clearly independent: for diffeomorphism-invariant metric theories the field equations should be divergence-free, and the real issue is still the missing proof of the tensor form in Ref. [19].
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Both other reviewers correctly identified the unavailable Ref. [19] as the central weakness behind Theorems 2 and 6.
The point that Theorems 6-7 are mostly conditional algebra once the almost-universality ansatz (44) is granted is valid.
The factorization/general-solution caveat around equations (59)-(60) is valid, though secondary.
The topological missing-partner observation is plausible but not enough to carry the main almost-universality claim without the missing proof.

main_claims.claim: The product background metric 2 du dv + h_ab dx^a dx^b with positive constant transverse curvature solves cosmological Einstein-Maxwell equations for A_mu = sqrt(2 Lambda/kappa) v lambda_mu.
main_claims.notes: The Einstein-tensor and stress-tensor matching is explicit in (16)-(19). The Maxwell equation is not written out; it appears to require the constancy of epsilon, which the text notes after (18).

main_claims.claim: For the background metric, every rank-two curvature tensor contribution in a generic gravity theory has the form e0 gbar_mu_nu + e1 h_mu_nu.
main_claims.notes: This is the central almost-universality assertion for the background, but the proof is entirely deferred to Ref. [19], listed as in preparation.

main_claims.claim: The Kerr-Schild metric with profile V(u,x^a) solves cosmological Einstein-Maxwell with null dust, with Phi = -(1/kappa) Box V.
main_claims.notes: The gravitational matching is displayed in (37)-(40). The physical null-dust interpretation still needs qualifications on conservation, regularity, and sign of Phi if the source is meant literally.

main_claims.claim: For any generic gravity theory, the Kerr-Schild metric reduces to Einstein-Maxwell with null dust and a finite linear scalar equation sum c_n Box^n V.
main_claims.notes: The algebra from (44) to (53) is plausible, but (44) is precisely the unproved almost-universality input from Ref. [19]. The finite-order operator assumption is also stronger than the broad wording around generic higher-derivative theories.

main_claims.claim: The background is Petrov type D and the Kerr-Schild metric is Petrov type II.
main_claims.notes: The type-D/type-II criteria are invoked with computed Weyl expressions, but exact type II should be qualified by possible special profiles, including V=0 where the metric reduces to the type-D background.

main_claims.claim: The quadratic and cubic gravity examples realize the proposed reduction explicitly.
main_claims.notes: These examples are useful evidence and are more concrete than the generic theorem, but I have not independently certified the tensor algebra. They also inherit some sign/reality qualifications for the effective Einstein-Maxwell reinterpretation.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The unpublished tensor classification in Ref. [19] applies exactly to the metrics, dimension, profile dependence, and source conventions used here.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: This is the bridge from the explicit examples to the claimed almost-universality for arbitrary higher-curvature field equations.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The gravitational theory has finite derivative order, yielding a finite polynomial in Box acting on V.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without finite order, the constants c_n, highest order N, and factorization into Klein-Gordon-type factors may not exist.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The Maxwell amplitude is constant and the source-free Maxwell equations hold in both the background and Kerr-Schild metrics.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Solving only the gravitational equations with a Maxwell stress tensor is not enough for an Einstein-Maxwell solution.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The profile V(u,x^a) is sufficiently regular, independent of the affine null coordinate v, and compatible with the global topology and boundary conditions on the transverse surface.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The local linear PDE reduction does not by itself ensure global smooth solutions or the claimed always-admits-a-solution behavior.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Negative or sign-changing Phi is acceptable, or else V is restricted so that the null dust satisfies an energy condition.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The physical interpretation as null dust depends on positivity and conservation, not merely algebraic tensor form.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The updated Einstein-Maxwell parameters define a real Maxwell field when the paper interprets the reduced equations physically.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Some higher-curvature examples permit negative transverse curvature; the corresponding effective Maxwell amplitude may be formal unless sign conventions are spelled out.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the tensor-basis classification asserted in Theorems 2 and 6 hold for all rank-two tensors built from curvature and covariant derivatives for these metrics?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The proof is not in the manuscript and Ref. [19] is unavailable, so the key invariant/tensor algebra cannot be audited from the provided text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the Petrov classifications exact, or should Theorem 8 state type II or more special under nondegeneracy assumptions on V?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Resolving this requires a detailed Newman-Penrose or invariant classification for special profiles beyond the short argument given around (55)-(58).

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the quadratic and cubic examples have consistent sign conventions for Lambda, kappa, epsilon, and the effective Maxwell amplitude Upsilon, especially for the negative-curvature H^2 branch?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The algebra is lengthy and convention-sensitive; a human specialist should verify whether the effective Einstein-Maxwell interpretation remains physical or only formal on those branches.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the Maxwell equations and null-dust conservation hold in the full Kerr-Schild metric for the stated profile class?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The check is likely short but absent from the paper; it should be verified with the exact coordinate and sign conventions.

novelty_assessment: The proposed extension from known flat/(A)dS Kerr-Schild-Kundt almost-universal metrics to pp-waves with constant-curvature transverse spaces is plausible and interesting. The connection with the product background R^{1,1} x S^2 and Einstein-Maxwell/null-dust systems is a nice organizing observation, though related product geometries are not wholly new. The strong novelty claim would be substantial if the Ref. [19] tensor classification were included or available; in the present manuscript the novelty is only partially demonstrated through examples and conditional reductions.
score_rationale: The idea is promising and the explicit examples provide nontrivial evidence, but the paper does not prove its central almost-universality theorem and relies on an unavailable in-preparation reference. Some objections raised by other reviewers are misreadings, so I do not drop the score as far as a fatal-error verdict. Still, as a standalone mathematics review artifact, the main theorem is under-supported and needs either the missing proof or a much narrower conditional framing.
summary: The paper proposes constant-curvature transverse pp/Kerr-Schild metrics as a new almost-universal class, reducing broad higher-curvature gravity equations to cosmological Einstein-Maxwell equations with null dust. After reading the other reviews and rechecking the displayed equations, I keep the main concern: the load-bearing almost-universality tensor classification is not proved here and is cited to an unavailable in-preparation reference. I also reject two stronger objections from other reviewers that appear to be misreadings of the equations. The manuscript is promising as an announcement with examples, but not yet a self-contained mathematics-style proof of the headline claim.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper explicitly says the relevant property was shown in Ref. [19], but Ref. [19] is listed as in preparation. The main claims in Theorems 3, 6, 7 and Corollaries 1-2 depend on this tensor-basis statement.
technical_objections.objection: The central almost-universality theorem is not proved in this manuscript.
technical_objections.paper_location: Theorem 2 and Theorem 6, especially the citations to Ref. [19] after Theorem 1 and before Theorem 6

technical_objections.evidence: The paper starts with a smooth function of curvature and derivatives, but later assumes a finite highest derivative order N and a finite polynomial in Box. Infinite derivative, nonlocal, or insufficiently regular theories are not covered by the displayed reduction.
technical_objections.objection: The phrase any generic gravity theory is broader than the assumptions actually stated.
technical_objections.paper_location: Generic action (6), Theorem 6 equation (44), and factorization equation (59)

technical_objections.evidence: The ansatz initially allows epsilon(u). Source-free Maxwell equations appear to force epsilon to be constant; the text obtains constancy from the algebraic gravitational equations but does not present the Maxwell-equation verification in Theorems 1 or 5.
technical_objections.objection: The Maxwell equations are not explicitly checked for the proposed Einstein-Maxwell solutions.

technical_objections.evidence: Phi is a differential expression in V and can change sign for general profiles. If the term null dust is intended as physical matter rather than a convenient rank-one tensor source, additional restrictions are needed.
technical_objections.objection: The null-dust source is treated formally, without positivity or conservation qualifications.
technical_objections.paper_location: Theorem 5, Theorem 7, equations (43), (48), (52)-(53), and Example 1 equation (70)

technical_objections.evidence: For V=0 the metric is the background already claimed to be type D. Other profiles with vanishing or special Omega_alpha_beta may also be more special, so the theorem should say type II or more special unless exact-type hypotheses are stated.
technical_objections.objection: The Petrov type-II theorem likely needs a nondegeneracy qualifier.

technical_objections.evidence: The factorization assumes a finite polynomial operator and distinct nonzero roots; the text only briefly notes repeated or zero roots and does not discuss real solution spaces for complex roots or global boundary conditions on S^2 or H^2.
technical_objections.objection: The general solution obtained by factorizing the higher-order scalar equation is stated too quickly.

technical_objections.evidence: The updated parameter Upsilon satisfies relations that can force Upsilon^2 proportional to Lambda. Yet the quadratic example allows negative Lambda/topology R^{1,1} x H^2. If Upsilon is to be a real Maxwell amplitude with standard sign kappa, this needs clarification.
technical_objections.objection: The effective Einstein-Maxwell reinterpretation needs sign and reality conditions on the updated electromagnetic amplitude.
